top_news.title: Emerging Currencies Weaken as Iran Conflict Dims Risk Appetite
top_news.summary: Most emerging-market currencies fell against the dollar as oil traded above $105 a barrel and tensions in the Middle East escalated, with President Donald Trump and Iran’s leadership rejecting each other’s peace proposals and signaling no end in sight to the 10-week-old war.

top_news.title: Circle Surges Amid Optimism Over Own Blockchain, Legislation
top_news.summary: Circle Internet Group Inc.’s shares jumped as much as 14% amid optimism over the prospects of the stablecoin issuer’s ARC blockchain project and as US lawmakers move toward considering a landmark digital asset bill.

top_news.title: Iran Says It’s Deployed Mini Subs in Contest to Control Hormuz
top_news.summary: Iran says it’s deployed small submarines to act as an “invisible guardian” of the Strait of Hormuz amid a series of rejected peace deals between Tehran and the US.

top_news.title: Fertilizer Maker Mosaic Loses Out as Iran War Roils Inputs
top_news.summary: The war in Iran has sent fertilizer prices soaring, but that’s not creating a windfall for producer Mosaic Co.

top_news.title: Hedge Funds Move In as Litigation Finance Assets Slump
top_news.summary: A losing streak in litigation finance is attracting hedge funds and other alternative investment managers looking to invest in legal claims at distressed valuations, according to people familiar with the matter.

top_news.title: SEC Moves to End ‘Gag Rule’ Criticized by Musk and Cuban
top_news.summary: The White House is reviewing a plan for the Securities and Exchange Commission to end its decades-old policy that lets companies and people settle enforcement actions without admitting wrongdoing if they also promise not to dispute the allegations.

top_news.title: Chile Signals Codelco Governance Overhaul Amid Debt Concerns
top_news.summary: Chile’s new right-wing government is signaling an overhaul of Codelco’s corporate governance to ensure greater scrutiny of decision making at the heavily indebted state copper giant.

top_news.title: Goldman, BofA Delay Fed Cut Calls After ‘Last Straw’ Jobs Data
top_news.summary: Goldman Sachs & Co and Bank of America Corp are the latest in a growing cohort of Wall Street banks pushing back their forecasts for interest-rate cuts, arguing that both jobs and inflation data make a case for the Federal Reserve to keep rates on hold until at least the end of the year.

top_news.title: Bond CEO: Private Jet Demand Set to Surge
top_news.summary: Bill Papariella, CEO of the private jet company Bond joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why the rich are about to fly even more, and why most aviation companies are getting it wrong.  Papariella breaks down the explosion in wealth, the hidden inefficiencies in private aviation, and how his company is betting billions on a more exclusive, less chaotic flying experience. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_news.title: Starz CEO: Niche Hits Drive Growth
top_news.summary: Starz CEO Jeffrey Hirsch breaks down how hits like Outlander are powering the company’s post-spinoff strategy. By targeting loyal niche audiences, leveraging big platforms like Amazon, and doubling down on franchise IP, Hirsch says Starz is proving you don’t need Netflix-scale to win in streaming. (Source: Bloomberg)
